fields.description:
## Why
Only PASSing completion verdicts leave a durable artifact (the signed attestation). FAIL verdicts vanish — .rebar/run_snapshots/ is empty and the 2026-07-12 calibration audit could only reconstruct FAILs from agents' voluntary session-log narration. The next completion-verifier calibration needs the actual verdicts: failing criterion, findings[], remediation text, timestamps.

## What
- On a completion-verifier FAIL in the close path (src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py, verdict produced via src/rebar/llm/completion.py reconcile_verdict), persist the full verdict durably — mirror the plan-review REVIEW_RESULT sidecar pattern (an event/sidecar in the ticket store keyed to the ticket, not a git-ignored local file), so FAILs are queryable offline like plan-review runs are.
- Include a `schema` version string `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"` (mirroring the existing sidecars' `plan_review_result_v1` / `code_review_result_v1`, whose readers guard on `payload.get("schema")` — see plan_review/sidecar.py:157) so the new record type is identifiable and version-guarded, plus: verdict, findings[] (criterion, finding/detail, remediation), certifiable, model/runner, timestamp, and the ticket's material fingerprint at verify time.
- Keep the close path's behavior unchanged (persist-and-block; a persistence failure must not mask the FAIL). Implement the persist call inside a best-effort try/except in `_completion_precheck` (transition_close.py) BEFORE the FAIL is raised, mirroring plan_review/sidecar.emit's best-effort contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A FAILed close attempt leaves a durable, queryable record (a reducer-ignored sidecar event in the ticket store, mirroring REVIEW_RESULT) carrying `schema="completion_verifier_fail_v1"`, the failing criteria, and per-finding remediation text.
- [ ] A test drives a mocked FAIL through the close path (monkeypatch rebar.llm.verify_completion → FAIL stub, mirror tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_completion_gate.py) and asserts the record exists with the expected fields (schema + criteria + remediation).
- [ ] A test exercises the PERSISTENCE-FAILURE branch: the sidecar write is mocked to raise, and the close path still blocks with the FAIL verdict unchanged (fail-closed preserved, persistence error swallowed) — asserting the exception does not mask or alter the FAIL.
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
